data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

OWNED CRITERIA — FULLY covered by this child:
- Parent AC3 ("The dual outbound applier paths are collapsed to one (the production path); the retired path is deleted") → 33d0 AC1 (exactly one applier path in production; retired path deleted, no dead separately-tested applier code). FULLY.
- Parent SC2 ("Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path") → 33d0 AC1 + AC2 (single multi-sub-op UPDATE — fields+comments+labels+links+parent — through the production path asserting each sub-op took effect). FULLY.
- Contributes to AC7 (33d0 AC3: no capability regresses; suite green) and SC5/AC6 posture (33d0 AC4: asserts effects, not interaction/mock-call-counts).

DELEGATION — criteria this child does not own are correctly assigned to named siblings; declared OUT item (sub-op telemetry → story E/2359) consistent with roster: AC1/AC2/SC1 → 8937 (schema SSOT + round-trip contract) + fe57 (fixtures/fake) + 59ef (verified-fake honesty); AC4/SC3 → 2359 (telemetry + silent-no-op canary); AC5/SC4 → 822a (hermetic bidirectional round-trip + de-encode 'expected-bug' guards). AC7 structural/aggregate roll-up.

No per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to child 33d0 itself; its owned AC3/SC2 are fully covered.

The single roster-level AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests — no sibling produces the documented governing artifact; each only applies the posture to its own tests) is already tracked across the sibling set and on this ticket; not re-filed here to avoid duplication.
